title: kvm-pilot firmware registry
description: Single source of truth for firmware currency. Each entry is keyed by (vendor, product) as normalized by a driver's get_firmware_info(), so one generic mechanism serves every family (PiKVM/GLKVM/Redfish/iDRAC/iLO/IPMI/AMT). Consumed by health.check_firmware_currency; maintained via the firmware-report ingestion pipeline.

vendor.description: Matched case-insensitively against get_firmware_info().vendor (e.g. gl.inet, dell, hpe).

product.description: Matched case-insensitively as a substring of get_firmware_info().product (e.g. RV1126B, iDRAC9, iLO 5).

latest.description: Latest known version, in the same scheme the device reports.

versions.description: Per-firmware-version rows. Their maturity is DERIVED from data/test_runs.jsonl by kvm_pilot.maturity (#98) — never hand-edit; CI re-derives and fails on drift.

version.description: The firmware version this row describes, in the device's own scheme (matches the ledger's firmware_version).

maturity.description: DERIVED from data/test_runs.jsonl by kvm_pilot.maturity (#98) — never hand-edit; CI re-derives and fails on drift.

level.description: Overall level for this combo: the minimum of the exercised capabilities' levels.

capabilities.description: Every capability exercised live for this combo, with its derived level.

profile.description: Capability / expected-UX differentiators that a healthcheck cannot safely detect live. All fields optional so a profile can be enriched incrementally.

mouse.description: GUI usability: absolute = usable pointer; relative = painful; none = keyboard only.

vmedia.description: Boot-from-ISO fidelity: reports-only = API says mounted but the host sees nothing (#77).

power_state_trusted.description: Whether ATX/LED power readings are truthful — governs whether reboots can be automated safely.

video.description: Rated video ceiling: transport + max mode, e.g. h264/1080p60.

remote_update.description: Per-model remote firmware-update descriptor. Drives the healthcheck's actionable offer and the `firmware-update` command's reliability assessment. Absent/supported=false means the model is detect-only (guide the operator to the vendor UI).

supported.description: Whether a driver can flash this model over the network at all.

method.description: How the flash is driven, e.g. gl-api (GL /api/upgrade/*), redfish-updateservice.

risk.description: Reliability of an unattended remote flash. high = vendor recommends physical presence / no remote recovery.

recovery_required.description: True when a failed flash needs physical access to recover (no out-of-band remote recovery).

self_flash_blind.description: True when the KVM flashes its own firmware and reboots, dropping the control channel used to observe it.

notes.description: Free-text caveats (e.g. update may disable the REST API; no A/B rollback).
